figures.caption: Fig. 1. Deep learning approaches in image reconstruction (a) without using the imaging model, (b,c) using it disjointly, and (d) jointly with data consistency in a plug-andplay (PnP) framework. (e) Our proposed iterative dual-domain denoising framework (DenOiS), alternating between observation and solution domain refinement. (f) Illustration on an ultrasound application for speed-of-sound imaging from incomplete and noisy observations with an inexact model.
Table 2. Real phantom data results reporting RMSE (ACE). Values within half a standard deviation of the best results 9.79±5.54 (13.48±4.0) are highlighted. Gray marks baseline configurations.
Table 1. In-silico results on (C2) reporting RMSE (ACE). Gray marks baseline configurations. Rows are observation and columns are reconstruction approaches.

Fig. 2. (a) Sample reconstructions for C2 and phantom data, with RMSE (ACE) values reported. (b) An in-vivo breast cancer example reporting ∆c / gCNR. (c) Corrections of two acquired observations with different MD variants.
